# Transcrição: 22. Trait 21 - Great Salespeople are convinced that their products are more valuable than money they

- **Fonte:** 22. Trait 21 - Great Salespeople are convinced that their products are more valuable than money they
- **Duração:** 01:25
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:25]

The great salespeople are convinced, thoroughly and completely convinced, maybe even brainwashed, somehow brainwashed themselves, that their products and services are more valuable than the money they charge for them. These people are fanatics. I'm telling you, they're insanely like all the way overboard with this idea that I gotta get my product into the hands of other people. Regardless of how many times somebody says it's too much, I can't afford it, we can't do it, we're on a budget, this guy's sitting there saying you have to have this product regardless, no matter what. See, they're sold. These people have a completely different view of their products than the people they compete against. They know their product, believe their product is worth many more times than price or money or whatever the customer has to do in order to get it, and they certainly know that their product is worth more than the competition can offer. They don't compete on price. Look, the greats don't even think about price as a valid objection. They compete on one thing, value, the value proposition. And in their mind, in their mind, they know their value proposition outweighs everyone else. Look, check out the book, Sell or Be Sold, if you want to learn more about how to build your value and proposition and quit giving your price up. Because the greats, the greats see it as a ridiculous thing to give up price when they have so much of value to offer their customers.
